name: Fritz Kaegi
bio: Fritz Kaegi is the incumbent Cook County Assessor and a candidate in the 2026 Democratic primary. The assessor's office oversees property assessment values used in county tax billing. (Source: scout ballot context, March 13, 2026)

jacket.note: County race — ILSBE filing, not FEC.

red_flags.label: Both candidates accuse each other of accepting questionable donations (Fox32 Feb
red_flags.detail: Both candidates accuse each other of accepting questionable donations (Fox32 Feb 2026 — mutual accusations, neither substantiated)

red_flags.label: Hynes campaign argues Kaegi's data errors caused chaotic assessment appeals and
red_flags.detail: Hynes campaign argues Kaegi's data errors caused chaotic assessment appeals and missed deadlines — contested claim

red_flags.label: Kaegi has loaned his own campaign $5.4M (debt obligation on ILSBE) — heavy self-
red_flags.detail: Kaegi has loaned his own campaign $5.4M (debt obligation on ILSBE) — heavy self-funding by a wealthy candidate

red_flags.label: Both candidates accuse each other of accepting questionable donations (Fox32 Feb 2026 — mutual accusations, neither substantiated)
Hynes campaign argues Kaegi's data errors caused chaotic assessment appeals and missed deadlines — contested claim
Kaegi has loaned his own campaign $5.4M (debt obligation on ILSBE) — heavy self-funding by a wealthy candidate

key_votes.bill: 2018: Defeated incumbent Joseph Berrios (implicated in pay-to-play) — ran on tra
key_votes.summary: 2018: Defeated incumbent Joseph Berrios (implicated in pay-to-play) — ran on transparency/anti-corruption platform

key_votes.bill: 2018-present: Implemented mass reassessment reforms using data-driven methods; U
key_votes.summary: 2018-present: Implemented mass reassessment reforms using data-driven methods; U of Chicago study (2024) found 'substantial progress in improving fairness of residential assessments'

key_votes.bill: 2022: Survived primary challenge from Kari Steele (MWRD President) 53.8% to 46.2
key_votes.summary: 2022: Survived primary challenge from Kari Steele (MWRD President) 53.8% to 46.2% — contested race reflecting industry pushback

key_votes.bill: 2022-2025: Launched Affordable Housing Program, automated senior/veteran exempti
key_votes.summary: 2022-2025: Launched Affordable Housing Program, automated senior/veteran exemption renewals, expanded field inspection staff

key_votes.bill: 2025: Cook County Assessor's Office released 2024 Chicago Reassessment Report sh
key_votes.summary: 2025: Cook County Assessor's Office released 2024 Chicago Reassessment Report showing equity improvements; expanded commercial valuation reform with PTAX Reform Group

key_votes.bill: Debts of $5.4M on committee suggest significant self-loan to campaign
key_votes.summary: Debts of $5.4M on committee suggest significant self-loan to campaign

career_history.highlight: Won on reform platform; overhauled assessment equity practices

policy_platform.position: Championed fair property assessment reform, reducing the longstanding 'regressivity' that over-taxed lower-value properties while undertaxing commercial real estate.
Created online appeal tracking, published assessment methodology, expanded data access. First assessor to publish detailed mass appraisal models.
Raised assessments on undervalued commercial and industrial properties; faced intense opposition from commercial real estate lobby.

social_pulse.summary: Positive among housing equity advocates and progressive media. Negative from commercial real estate and property tax appeal attorneys. Moderate name recognition overall.

trust_indicators.label: No confirmed corruption or ethics findings

news_hits.headline: Cook County assessor candidates grapple over the reasons for skyrocketing property taxes
news_hits.title: Cook County assessor candidates grapple over the reasons for skyrocketing property taxes
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune pre-primary deep-dive on the Kaegi vs. Hynes race. Both candidates spar over who bears responsibility for soaring Cook County property tax bills. Kaegi defends his equalization reforms; Hynes argues those reforms shifted burdens onto homeowners.

news_hits.headline: Property tax collection rates drop in Chicago neighborhoods with big bill hikes
news_hits.title: Property tax collection rates drop in Chicago neighborhoods with big bill hikes
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune reports declining property tax collection rates in neighborhoods with the largest bill increases. Notes that several community leaders in hard-hit neighborhoods have endorsed challenger Pat Hynes over incumbent Kaegi, citing the burden of assessment increases.

news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive March 17 primary preview covers all key races including Cook County Board President (Preckwinkle vs. Reilly), Cook County Assessor (Kaegi vs. Hynes), U.S. Senate, governor, and comptroller.

news_hits.title: What elections are coming up in Illinois? Here's what's ahead in the 2026 primary
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago overview of key 2026 primary races, covering Preckwinkle vs. Reilly for Cook County Board President and Kaegi vs. Hynes for Assessor among the notable local contests.

news_hits.title: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi faces strong challenge from suburban assessor Pat Hynes in bid for 3rd term
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times profiles the Kaegi vs. Hynes assessor primary. Hynes, first-term Lyons Township Assessor with 23 years working inside the Assessor's office as a residential field inspector, is running as the Cook County Democratic Party-endorsed challenger against Kaegi's third-term bid. The piece covers their contrasting approaches to property valuation methodology and accountability.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary: What to know for Tuesday's election
news_hits.summary: Tribune day-before guide covers the Assessor's race, showing Kaegi and Hynes at a Des Plaines candidate forum. Race framed as incumbent-defender vs. property-tax-reform challenger with one day to go.

news_hits.title: Cold weather to greet Illinois voters Tuesday as primary features open US Senate, crowded House races
news_hits.summary: Tribune election-eve (March 16) roundup confirms cold weather forecast for primary day. Cook County Assessor race between incumbent Kaegi and challenger Hynes is among the key county-level contests voters will decide Tuesday. Both candidates have made skyrocketing property tax bills the central flashpoint of the race.

news_hits.title: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi faces suburban opponent for influential position over property taxes
news_hits.summary: Fritz Kaegi faces Pat Hynes in Democratic primary; Kaegi previously won 82%+ against Libertarian in 2022. Winner faces Libertarian Nico Tsatsoulis in November.

news_hits.title: 2026 primary day election results: Cook County assessor
news_hits.summary: Two-term Assessor Fritz Kaegi faces a challenge from Pat Hynes in the Democratic primary.

news_hits.title: Election Night: Kaegi vs. Hynes Cook County Assessor results being counted
news_hits.summary: Results are being tallied in the hotly contested Cook County Assessor Democratic primary on Election Night. Two-term incumbent Fritz Kaegi faces challenger Pat Hynes (Lyons Township assessor). Since there are no Republican candidates running for assessor, the Democratic primary winner will automatically be the next assessor. Kaegi has blasted Hynes for accepting donations from property tax attorneys; Hynes blames Kaegi for skyrocketing property taxes on the South and West sides of Chicago.

news_hits.title: Pat Hynes defeats Assessor Fritz Kaegi, as Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle wins Democratic primary
news_hits.summary: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi conceded to challenger Pat Hynes in the Democratic primary. Kaegi, first elected in 2018, was ousted after an eight-year tenure that included a controversial property reassessment model and the troubled Tyler Technologies tax billing system rollout.

news_hits.title: In other closely watched races, La Shawn Ford won the Democratic primary for 7th Congressional District and longtime Cook County assessor Fritz Kaegi will soon be out after losing to Pat Hynes
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times election night wrap notes that longtime Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi will soon be out of office after losing to challenger Pat Hynes in the Democratic primary.

news_hits.title: Two incumbents fall, appointees hold on in Cook County Board races
news_hits.summary: Cook County primary roundup noting Fritz Kaegi fell to Pat Hynes in the assessor race while Toni Preckwinkle won the county board president race; two incumbent county board members also lost.

news_hits.title: Super PAC scorecard — how outside spending groups fared in efforts to influence Illinois primary voters
news_hits.summary: WBEZ post-primary PAC analysis notes that Fritz Kaegi, who lost to Pat Hynes in the Cook County Assessor Democratic primary, was outpowered by the Cook County Democratic Party's organizational machine. The race was a test of whether Kaegi's reform-oriented legacy could survive a well-organized slate challenge backed by assessor industry insiders and the party establishment.

news_hits.title: Cook County Assessor Candidate Pat Hynes on Property Taxes, His Vision for the Office
news_hits.summary: Post-primary WTTW interview with Pat Hynes following Fritz Kaegi's concession, covering Hynes's vision for the assessor's office and approach to property taxes.

news_hits.title: More than $30M in outside spending shaped Illinois primaries, but wins were a split for big-monied interests
news_hits.summary: Tribune post-primary analysis of $30M+ in outside spending. Crypto-industry PAC spent $10M against Stratton in the Senate race but failed. The Cook County Assessor race saw significant outside money back Kaegi (Tribune editorial board endorsed him) while Hynes, backed by the Cook County Democratic Party, prevailed decisively.

news_hits.title: Two-Term Incumbent Fritz Kaegi Concedes to Pat Hynes in Cook County Assessor's Race
news_hits.summary: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi conceded defeat to Pat Hynes on election night, a one-time employee of Kaegi's own office. Hynes's win ends Kaegi's eight-year tenure at the assessor's office. No Republican filed for the assessor seat, making Hynes the likely next Cook County Assessor.

news_hits.title: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi lost big where property taxes in city, suburbs soared
news_hits.summary: Post-primary analysis shows Kaegi lost worst in precincts hit hardest by rising property taxes, as homeowners blamed the assessor's office for assessment methodology that drove up tax bills. His strongest support came from precincts with lower tax bill increases. The analysis affirms that property tax frustration was the decisive factor in his defeat.

news_hits.title: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi lost big where property taxes in city, suburbs soared
news_hits.summary: WBEZ analysis mirrors Sun-Times findings: Kaegi's primary loss was geographically driven by property tax angst — he performed worst in areas of Cook County where homeowners faced the steepest tax increases. Hynes, as Lyons Township Assessor, benefited from comparison as a local official closer to suburban homeowner frustrations.

news_hits.title: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson's budget director got illegal property tax breaks for five years
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times Watchdogs investigation reveals Mayor Brandon Johnson's budget director Annette Guzman received illegal homeowner property tax exemptions for five years on a South Loop condo she no longer lived in — breaks administered by Kaegi's office, which also employed Guzman as chief administrative officer until 2020. Kaegi's staff failed to process Guzman's 2021 request to cancel the exemption, resulting in $3,434 in wrongful tax breaks. After Sun-Times inquiries, Kaegi ordered Guzman to repay only three years' worth ($2,071.89) and waived interest — an outcome critics say underscores the lax oversight that plagued Kaegi's assessor tenure in its final stretch.
